body.title: Audit queue for observe/wait tickets without explicit trip conditions
body.result: Shipped in 23910757. Audit tool lab/claudecode/audit_immobile_tickets.py sweeps pending/blocked/in_progress/awaiting_approval tickets for observe/wait/monitor shape, classifies MOBILE (has trip condition: TTL/count/event/companion/body-explicit) vs IMMOBILE. Today's report: 49 tickets scanned, 4 observe-shape candidates, 0 immobile — queue hygiene clean. Re-runnable for future audits.
body.description:
Sweep the existing ticket queue for observe/wait/monitor-shaped tickets that lack an explicit trip condition, per feedback_no_immobile_observe_tickets.md. Shape to look for: body text like 'observe', 'wait for', 'monitor until', 'run and see', 'check back', or 'see if it's still a problem' — without a concrete next-move trigger.

For each match: either (a) add a trip condition (TTL, count threshold, traversal count, event gate, companion ticket, or body-explicit next-move spec) via /ticket update; or (b) if the observation is no longer relevant, close the ticket with a reason. No ticket should remain immobile in the queue.

Scope: review-and-annotate pass across existing tickets in clan.memories where parent_id='TICKETS_ROOT' and status IN ('pending','blocked'). Produce a report (lab/design_docs/queue_hygiene/immobile_tickets_2026-04-21.md or similar) listing the audit outcome per ticket.

Context: part of pre-Pass-3 cleanup. Pass 3 cannot begin until all addressable tickets resolve, so immobile-ticket hygiene matters. Akien 2026-04-21.

test_plan: after the audit, re-run the same grep/filter and confirm zero immobile tickets remain. Any remaining matches must have been deliberately left with an explicit next-move note in the body.
